findings.root_cause: Project governance discovery calls is_dir before rejecting dangling or non-directory symlink components.
findings.correction: Inspect every lexical governance component before metadata or enumeration and fail closed.

validation.evidence: Focused Stage-2 suite rejects dangling/live links, wrong types, inaccessible components, and premature bundled discovery; real-link cases remain scheduled for Linux CI.

findings.root_cause: Profile and module registries use separate identity maps and lock verification collapses selected packs by id.
findings.correction: Enforce one global pack identity namespace and reject duplicate selected identities before lock construction.

validation.evidence: Profile/module id, name, and cross-field collision permutations fail deterministically before registry or lock dictionary collapse; generated locks self-verify.

findings.root_cause: Approval normalization validates accountable_authority type but does not apply intrinsic non-human identity denial.
findings.correction: Reject exact and prefixed non-human authority identities during normalization.

validation.evidence: Direct normalization, pack loading, programmatic composition, v2 serialization, retained-source verification, and document evaluation reject malformed and explicit non-human authority.

findings.root_cause: Retained-source verification uses the untrusted claimed normalized id as its own fallback.
findings.correction: Derive identity independently from raw source and a documented source-path fallback.

validation.evidence: Changed/deleted identity, conflicting shape, changed path, case, whitespace and Unicode-confusable mutations all fail v2 re-derivation. Missing raw identity is derived only from shape/path; arbitrary in-band fallbacks fail even with a recomputed consistency binding. The frozen v1 serializer/schema remains valid.

findings.root_cause: Invalidation conditions are converted with str instead of validated as source strings.
findings.correction: Strictly validate non-empty unique strings and mark malformed approval input invalid.

validation.evidence: Boolean, mapping, integer, empty, whitespace and duplicate invalidation inputs return resolution=invalid with APPROVAL_INVALIDATION_CONDITION_INVALID. Roles, evidence, actions, governed revisions, exception authorities, rule references, evidence dependencies and closure evidence are checked as source strings and are never stringified.

findings.root_cause: The standalone human-approval check requires exact=true but does not compare governed subject revisions.
findings.correction: Resolve one canonical governed revision and compare all approval/evidence/change/architecture bindings.

validation.evidence: One independent top-level subject revision is required; approval, evidence-record, change, architecture and architecture-evidence claims are compared without relying on change_control selection.

findings.root_cause: High-risk change authorization accepts any role intersection instead of a strict authorized subset.
findings.correction: Require every declared role and irreversible authority to be eligible, human, non-denied and sufficient.

validation.evidence: Every high-risk role must be a unique canonical human role in one candidate's eligible set, include all required roles, avoid denials, and match the same revision/scope; irreversible authority is checked independently.

findings.root_cause: The separation-of-duties actors schema has no minimum and structural checks are vacuous for an empty approver set.
findings.correction: Require and independently validate at least one unique human approver, join each change assignment to every restrictive approval gate and its connected passing evidence, and reject any required-independence overlap.

validation.evidence: Empty, scalar, mapping, padded, duplicate, semantic-duplicate, explicit non-human and case-varied approvers fail without exceptions; malformed enum values return diagnostics instead of raising. Canonical change subjects, risk, author, role, restrictive multi-gate and connected-evidence joins are enforced. Declared or actual producer overlap fails when evidence independence is required, including forged approval-type labels; the module declares both required block schemas.

findings.root_cause: Exported path APIs default the trust boundary to the caller-supplied root, omitting higher ancestors.
findings.correction: Derive an independent lexical trust root or require a safe explicit one without breaking callers.

validation.evidence: Direct registry, directory, pack, byte-reader, and evidence APIs inspect from an independent anchor; explicit roots only narrow containment.

findings.root_cause: Lock loading uses unrestricted Path.read_text and permissive json.loads.
findings.correction: Use the bounded hardened local-file loader, strict UTF-8, duplicate-key rejection and stable lock diagnostics.

validation.evidence: Bounded strict lock reads reject links, traversal, oversized/invalid UTF-8, duplicate keys, non-finite JSON, schema errors, directories, and unsafe writes.

findings.root_cause: Remote-path checks cover URL schemes but not Windows UNC and device namespaces.
findings.correction: Centrally reject UNC, device and extended Windows paths before any filesystem call.

validation.evidence: Host-independent URI, UNC, extended/device namespace and DOS-device matrix is rejected before mocked lstat/exists probes across public APIs and CLIs.

findings.root_cause: Composition replaces complete retained approval sources with path-only merged_sources metadata.
findings.correction: Introduce bounded deterministic effective-approval provenance, registry-backed source authentication, and independent recomputation verification under explicit versioned serializers.

validation.evidence: Bounded flattened v2 leaves, source hashes, deterministic provenance order and declared operations are replayed by the public verifier. Built-ins resolve only from packaged packs; other tiers require an explicit registry. Forged pack lineage, contradictory or duplicate id/index/path lineage, over-limit generators, excessive nesting, document reuse, source/recipe/result mutations, disjoint roles and excluded requirements fail.

findings.root_cause: Exception closure is enforced only for closed status and active interval overlap is not checked.
findings.correction: Use order-independent half-open lifecycle validation, require passing closure proof, reject intersecting active control/scope/time intervals, and require explicit non-overlapping renewals with exact-type, authority-bound, unique, predecessor-fresh human-approval evidence plus a renewal-action gate requiring the exact same evidence set. Only passing evidence dependency chains may satisfy governance references; selected built-in controls and rules cannot be exception targets.

validation.evidence: Missing/reversed intervals, malformed status values without crashes, premature active/expired states, stale active declarations, missing closure, same-control scope/time overlap, prohibited/dangling/self/cyclic/forked/overlapping renewals, mistyped/relabelled/reused/unknown/failed/post-activation approval proof, gate laundering, reserved governance diagnostic targets, and selected built-in rule/control targets fail. Adjacent intervals and independently preapproved renewals pass; failed, inconclusive, observed, or transitively failed evidence confers no authority.

findings.root_cause: Raw-byte-hashed evidence and architecture artifacts are treated as Git text and converted by autocrlf.
findings.correction: Mark every raw-byte-hashed artifact as non-text and validate a fresh autocrlf checkout.

validation.evidence: The protected inventory covers all governance evidence, architecture reports, direct compatibility JSON, generated-drift JSON, and pre-existing binary starter goldens. A real autocrlf clone compares checkout bytes with immutable HEAD blobs, runs both hash-bound examples, resolve/explain/matrix, and the compatibility corpus without weakening raw-byte hash verification.

findings.root_cause: Governed-package validation delegates legacy change id/type strings to a newly narrowed shared identity schema.
findings.correction: Preserve the complete base governed-package string and extension domain with a frozen compatibility adapter; enforce the strict shared model only for an explicitly selected top-level change-control block.

validation.evidence: Reported cases plus empty, whitespace, long, punctuation-leading, newline, emoji, CJK, arbitrary extension, and 250 seeded mutation values per field match the frozen 1.5.2 schema oracle with no narrowing or broadening. Missing and non-string base-invalid shapes still fail, while strict generalized change identities remain fail-closed.

findings.root_cause: Required fields were inserted into exported dataclass constructors and existing to_dict shapes changed.
findings.correction: Restore base-valid constructors with defaults and preserve legacy serialization through compatible output methods.

validation.evidence: Every base field remains an unchanged positional prefix; additive fields are trailing defaults; complete normalized-approval and composition v1 payloads, nested consumer mappings, retained source and diagnostics match the base contract; base public exports remain a subset; the isolated wheel smoke executes the legacy positional consumer.

findings.root_cause: Closure, security, compatibility and release records claim assurances contradicted by exact-head behavior.
findings.correction: Mark inaccurate records superseded immediately and regenerate exact-head claims only after independent validation.

validation.evidence: Current records asserted resolved behavior that adversarial execution disproved.

findings.root_cause: Reserved namespace validation rejects descendants but not exact nornyx.builtin equality.
findings.correction: Reject exact and descendant reserved namespace identities for every non-built-in tier.

validation.evidence: Exact root and descendant identities fail for project, org and explicit tiers in profile/module inputs; local byte/file loaders, forged dataclasses, and uncatalogued resources cannot claim bundled tier while authentic packaged packs remain registerable.

findings.root_cause: PACK_SCHEMA_INVALID is omitted from lock error classification.
findings.correction: Map all schema and semantic lock validation failures to documented exit code 2.

validation.evidence: Resolve, explain, matrix and profiles resolve return 2 in text and JSON modes for path, encoding, JSON, schema and semantic lock failures; schema-invalid packs remain ordinary exit 1 and check remains exit 1.

findings.root_cause: Migration tests assert metadata presence without proving the immutable old artifact or exact diff.
findings.correction: Commit immutable before/after artifacts and mechanically verify both hashes and the exact structural diff.

validation.evidence: Five migrations bind canonical immutable before/after bytes, exact JSON-pointer operations or unified text diff, proof-record hash, approval/reason/changelog metadata, closed artifact inventory, ordered chains and current terminal output.

findings.root_cause: Wheel smoke omits no-index/socket denial and reports network_used as a literal false value.
findings.correction: Install with no-index, clear network environment and derive network_used from an enforced socket-denial observation.

validation.evidence: The isolated smoke clears index/proxy configuration, uses pip --no-index/--no-deps, injects a socket-denial guard, fails on recorded address-resolution or connection attempts, and derives network_used from the attempt log while exercising imports, resources, legacy consumers and CLIs.

findings.root_cause: Validation used a clean-worktree diff check instead of the candidate base-to-head range.
findings.correction: Remove candidate whitespace defects and run the base-to-head check in CI/release assurance.

validation.evidence: Known candidate whitespace defects are removed and Linux CI runs git diff --check from audited base 95952226999327458c6fea81cb32d82539bcae5b through HEAD.
